coaching.coaching: Pin down post-merge fast-forward failure recovery behaviour with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: post-merge fast-forward failure recovery behaviour

coaching.coaching: Plan how CONTRIBUTING.md landing recipe insertion point will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: CONTRIBUTING.md landing recipe insertion point

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] acceptance criteria completeness evidence will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria completeness evidence

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

evidence: verify command `grep -n 'merge --ff-only origin/main' AGENTS.md` greps the unanchored word 'merge' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_merge'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"merge":')
verify command `grep -ni 'shared main checkout' AGENTS.md` greps the unanchored word 'shared' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_shared'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"shared":')

verify command `grep -n 'merge --ff-only origin/main' CONTRIBUTING.md` greps the unanchored word 'merge' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_merge'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"merge":')
verify command `grep -ni 'shared main checkout' CONTRIBUTING.md` greps the unanchored word 'shared' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_shared'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"shared":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback clause to the proposed `git merge --ff-only origin/main` instruction specifying what an agent should do if the command fails (e.g., 'if --ff-only is refused because the worktree has local commits, the worktree is not in a clean fast-forwardable state — do not force-reset; instead, inspect with `git status` / `git log origin/main..HEAD` and resolve before closing').
evidence: Plan §1 proposes: 'git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main from inside the worktree' with no branching on failure.
git merge --ff-only exits non-zero if the worktree tip is not an ancestor of origin/main (e.g., agent made a local commit before running this); no recovery path is specified.
AGENTS.md §7 / CONTRIBUTING.md §7 documents the affirmative-framing and completeness bar that agent-facing instructions must meet, making this an in-scope gap for an agent-readable doc.
findings.finding: The plan adds an agent-facing instruction (`git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main`) to AGENTS.md with no fallback specified for the case where the command fails — e.g., when the worktree has local commits that make fast-forward impossible (`--ff-only` refuses and exits non-zero). An LLM agent following this guidance has no specified next step on failure, which is a T8(d) structural gap in the instruction set.
scenarios: An agent has local commits in the worktree (e.g., a mid-edit state) and runs `git merge --ff-only origin/main`; it fails; the agent has no documented fallback and may stall or improvise incorrectly.
A worktree was branched from a slightly stale origin/main and a new commit was landed on main concurrently; ff-only fails; no recovery specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the proposed bullet: 'If `--ff-only` is refused (the worktree has local commits not on `origin/main`), STOP — do not force-reset; run `git log origin/main..HEAD` to confirm those commits belong to this change, then proceed with the ticket close only if the `rebar-ticket:` commit is already reachable (i.e., the merge has already landed and you have a newer local commit on top).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Plan §2 that §2f is a **new** subsection to be created immediately after the existing §2e (Submit) in CONTRIBUTING.md — there is no pre-existing §2f or 'landing recipe' label to locate.
evidence: Plan says: 'CONTRIBUTING.md — add the same guidance to the landing recipe (a §2f after Submit)'
CONTRIBUTING.md has §2a through §2e; §2e is 'Submit' (line 146); there is no §2f and no section labeled 'landing recipe'
ABSENCE: search for '§2f' and 'landing recipe' returned no matches in the repo
findings.finding: CONTRIBUTING.md has no existing '§2f' subsection and no section labeled 'landing recipe'; the plan's insertion label '§2f after Submit' refers to a new subsection to be created after §2e (Submit, line 146). This is fine as a creation, but the plan's phrasing ('add the same guidance to the landing recipe (a §2f after Submit)') may mislead an implementer into searching for an existing §2f or a section titled 'landing recipe' — neither of which exists.
scenarios: An implementer searches for an existing '§2f' or 'landing recipe' section, fails to find it, and is unsure where to insert the new content.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan text to: 'CONTRIBUTING.md — insert a new §2f ("Close the ticket") immediately after the existing §2e ("Submit") section, containing the same two-part fast-forward-your-worktree guidance and shared-main-checkout warning.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the Scope or Plan section explaining WHY docs-only is preferred (e.g., 'A code-layer fix would add a network call to every close, contradicting the established `fetch=False` offline-first close philosophy; the root cause is a behavioral gap addressable at zero runtime cost through documentation.').
evidence: Plan states 'DOCUMENTATION ONLY. No src/rebar code changes' without explaining why this is preferred over a code fix.

transition_close.py line 184: `fetch=False` and inline comment 'fetching the real origin on every close would add latency and a failure surface' — this is the positive rationale that justifies docs-only, but it is absent from the plan itself.
ABSENCE: The plan has no sentence of the form 'docs is preferred because …' or 'a code fix would be worse because …'
findings.finding: The plan declares its approach as 'DOCUMENTATION ONLY' in the Scope section but provides no affirmative rationale explaining WHY documentation is the right fix rather than a code-layer change (e.g., that a code fix would add a network call to every close, contradicting the deliberate `fetch=False` / offline-first close philosophy already established in `_completion_precheck` at line 184, and that the root cause is a behavioral gap not an architectural one). The absence of a positive rationale for the chosen approach is a G6 criterion gap.
findings.location: Plan section: Scope ('DOCUMENTATION ONLY. No src/rebar code changes; no new flag/parameter on transition/close.')
scenarios: An implementer reviewing this plan cannot distinguish 'docs-only because the author didn't consider a code fix' from 'docs-only because a code fix has specific downsides'. Without the rationale, a reviewer (or a future plan-review gate) cannot confirm the approach was chosen deliberately.
A follow-on contributor may propose a code guard unaware that the offline-first philosophy was a deliberate constraint, leading to rework.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, append: 'Rationale for docs-only: a code fix (e.g., implicitly fetching origin in `_referencing_commit_exists`, or adding a pre-close guard) would add network latency and a remote-availability failure surface to every close — directly contradicting the deliberate `fetch=False` offline-first posture established in `_completion_precheck` (line 184). The failure mode is a behavioral gap (agents not fast-forwarding their own worktree), not an architectural one, so documentation at zero runtime cost is the correct fix.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
